title: Symantec Endpoint Protection Blocked Malware by Risk and Host
dataSources: SEPM client risk logs via `sourcetype=symantec:ep:risk:file` (dump file or supported forwarder collection)
app: Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection (Splunkbase 2772)
spl:
index=endpoint sourcetype="symantec:ep:risk:file" earliest=-24h
| eval action=coalesce(Actual_Action, actual_action, "")
| where action="Blocked" OR action="Quarantined" OR action="Cleaned"
| stats count by Risk_Name, Computer_Name, action
| sort -count
description: Aggregating SEP risk events where the agent actively blocked, quarantined, or cleaned malware validates signature effectiveness and highlights persistent strains hitting specific machines.
value: Shows which malware families are most common in successful blocks and which hosts see repeat detections, guiding reimaging or policy tightening without waiting for weekly SEPM reports.
implementation: Deploy TA-sep14 (Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection) and ingest Client Risk logs from SEPM dumps or a supported forwarder path—avoid lossy UDP syslog for production. Map `Computer_Name` to CMDB. Tune `Actual_Action` values for your locale. Extend with threat intel lookups on `Risk_Name` or hash fields if extracted.
visualization: Bar chart (detections by Risk_Name), table (host × action), map or pie by action.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection
About the Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection

knownFalsePositives: Symantec block counts can jump during full scans, definition pushes, or when an admin walks EICAR for testing — compare to the console’s own totals.
grandmaExplanation: We track blocked malware in Symantec by risk and host so the worst clusters get attention and quiet corners do not hide failures.
